title: arundarbor aristata
intro: Arundarbor aristata, commonly known as the 'Bhui Amla' or 'Dhava', is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and other parts of Asia, scientifically classified as Anogeissus latifolia, although some sources consider it Anogeissus aristata. It is known for its medicinal properties and is used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine. The plant is believed to possess several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Arundarbor aristata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic actions, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities.
side_effects: Arundarbor aristata can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingestion of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and respiratory problems. Prolonged exposure to its sap can cause contact dermatitis and photosensitivity, making the skin more susceptible to sunburn. If not handled correctly, arundarbor aristata can also cause neurological symptoms such as headaches, dizziness, and tremors.
